title: Announcement: full port to csharp managed code
author: herbat73
body:
hi folks, 

small announcements.
 
I did port all source code for full managed code c#
No more bindings to c are necessary.

https://github.com/herbat73/libharu_csharp_port

title: Financing  Improvements To Libharu
author: dzucchetti
body:
We would like to sincerely thank the group of people who are currently fixing bugs and improving the library.

There are quite a lot of open issues, but unfortunately we do not have enough PDF-specific expertise to contribute directly by working on the code. Our company already supports other open source projects financially and we would certainly be open to providing financial support to the libaru project as well. There may also be others interested in contributing financially, if so, please let us know here.

We would also like to understand if there is someone willing to dedicate time to libaru, not only for development, but also for planning and coordinating tasks, reviewing code, and helping define priorities.

Ideally, it would be very useful to have a shared roadmap outlining how libaru could be improved, what the most important issues are, and which areas should be addressed first.

Please feel free to reply to this issue with any feedback, ideas, or interest in contributing, in any form.

title: Love coding, hate cross-compiling Linux -> Windows
author: Vantskruv
body:
Anyone successfully crosscompiled a Windows executable in Linux?
If I succeed, I will post the solution here.

My project, using libharu, is compiling great on Linux, but it does not work well compiling it for Windows in Linux.
Currently, I am not able to install windows on my machines at home, maybe in a couple of months or so.

title: PNG image not filling the page
author: RWAP

title: Build error for Win32 (x86) due to modifier mismatch
author: dgobbi
body:
I encountered a build error with Visual Studio 2019 (x86 native, platform=Win32),

```text
F:\libharu\src\hpdf_pdfa.c(148,1):
error C2373: 'HPDF_PDFA_SetPDFAConformance': redefinition; different type modifiers
```

There is a mismatch between the declarations in the include file vs. the source file

The definition in [include/hpdf_pdfa.h](../blob/master/include/hpdf_pdfa.h#L34) looks correct:
```text
HPDF_EXPORT(HPDF_STATUS)
HPDF_PDFA_SetPDFAConformance(HPDF_Doc pdf,
                             HPDF_PDFAType pdfatype);
```

The definition in [src/hpdf_pdfa.c](../blob/master/src/hpdf_pdfa.c#L147) needs to be modified to match:
```diff
 /* Set PDF/A conformance */
-HPDF_STATUS
+HPDF_EXPORT(HPDF_STATUS)
 HPDF_PDFA_SetPDFAConformance (HPDF_Doc pdf, HPDF_PDFAType pdfatype)
```

Note that this `HPDF_EXPORT` was first added in #309, but was removed (by accident?) in #316.

title: Cannot set the same owner and user password
author: guillaume-tgl
body:
libharu prevents from using the same user and owner password: https://github.com/libharu/libharu/blob/master/src/hpdf_encryptdict.c#L203
Is there any valid reason behind this choice? I couldn't find anything in the specifications that prevents from having the same password.

title: failure to build with LIBHPDF_DEBUG_TRACE on
author: lhf
body:
Turning LIBHPDF_DEBUG_TRACE on in hpdf_config.h with

`#define LIBHPDF_DEBUG_TRACE 1
`

gives this compilation error:

```
hpdf_shading.c:100:3: error: implicit declaration of function 'HPDF_PRINTF' is invalid in C99 [-Werror,-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
  HPDF_PTRACE((" HPDF_Shading_New\n"));
./hpdf_utils.h:153:28: note: expanded from macro 'HPDF_PTRACE'
#define HPDF_PTRACE(ARGS)  HPDF_PRINTF ARGS

hpdf_shading.c:189:3: error: implicit declaration of function 'HPDF_PRINTF' is invalid in C99 [-Werror,-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
  HPDF_PTRACE((" HPDF_Shading_AddVertexRGB\n"));
./hpdf_utils.h:153:28: note: expanded from macro 'HPDF_PTRACE'
#define HPDF_PTRACE(ARGS)  HPDF_PRINTF ARGS
```

This only happens for hpdf_shading.c. Apparently, it does not see a definition of HPDF_PRINTF.

The fix is to add this in hpdf_shading.c:

`#include "hpdf_conf.h"`

title: HPDF_FAILD_TO_ALLOC_MEM missing
author: lhf
body:
In 2.4.5, HPDF_FAILD_TO_ALLOC_MEM was renamed to HPDF_FAILED_TO_ALLOC_MEM, which broke builds.
Perhaps add this in hpdf_error.h for the transition:

`#define HPDF_FAILD_TO_ALLOC_MEM HPDF_FAILED_TO_ALLOC_MEM
`

Also, HPDF_FAILD_TO_ALLOC_MEM is still mentioned in comments:

```
libharu-2.4.5/src/hpdf_list.c:71: *           HPDF_FAILD_TO_ALLOC_MEM is returned when the expansion of the
libharu-2.4.5/src/hpdf_list.c:106: *           HPDF_FAILD_TO_ALLOC_MEM is returned when the expansion of the
```

title: Apple Frameworks
author: Treata11
body:
Greetings,

I noticed that the project's CMake minimum version is set to 3.10, which is not sufficient for Apple platform builds with CMake's official toolchain.
https://cmake.org/cmake/help/latest/manual/cmake-toolchains.7.html#id27

I took a look at the source & couldn't find [Framework properties](https://cmake.org/cmake/help/latest/prop_tgt/FRAMEWORK.html#prop_tgt:FRAMEWORK) set in project's configs...
I'd be happy to submit a pull request to add the required properties for appropriate targets.
